title: Define a bounded Percentage type
scenario: A percentage should not be represented as any arbitrary integer throughout the codebase.
instructions: Define Percentage, PercentageError, value, and TryFrom<u16> that rejects values above 100.

hints.body: Keep the Table-driven domain tests code active and define Percentage, PercentageError, value, and TryFrom<u16> that rejects values above 100.
Use the Rust API named in this lesson instead of adding a side helper; the previous arc code should stay in the main solution.
One complete solution for Define a bounded Percentage type is shown below.

completionExplanation: Define a bounded Percentage type now extends the active Table-driven domain tests code with percentage newtype. The previous lesson's behavior remains in the main solution rather than an archived module.
